dashboard.readyTitle: Ready to find your next location?
dashboard.readyDesc: Run a new scan to get instant market intelligence for any area.

welcome.heroTitle: Find the Perfect Spot for Your Next
welcome.heroDesc: Localyze aggregates foot traffic patterns, competitor density, and real-time demand signals into one clear answer — before you sign the lease.
welcome.searchPlaceholder: Enter a city, neighborhood, or address...
welcome.dataAggDesc: Scanning 20,000+ local points of interest and real-time foot traffic signals around any address.
welcome.aiAnalysisDesc: Evaluating competitor density, market saturation, and demand signals using multi-factor intelligence models.
welcome.actionableDesc: Delivering a confident Feasibility Score and location report you can act on — instantly.
welcome.capabilitiesTitle: Everything you need to choose right.
welcome.capabilitiesDesc: Three powerful lenses on any location — unified in one seamless analysis flow.
welcome.footTrafficDesc: Visualize pedestrian and vehicle flow patterns throughout the day and week — pinpoint peak visibility before you commit.
welcome.competitorDensityDesc: Map nearby competitors, understand their clustering, and gauge potential market pressure at any address you're evaluating.
welcome.marketSaturationDesc: Discover oversaturated zones and untapped pockets with our saturation index — move where others haven't looked yet.
welcome.insightDesc: Location analytics that used to cost thousands — now at your fingertips.
welcome.aboutDesc: A platform born from the belief that every entrepreneur deserves enterprise-grade location intelligence.
welcome.missionTitle: Making smart location decisions accessible to every business owner.
welcome.missionDesc: Choosing where to open a business is one of the most consequential decisions an entrepreneur can make. Localyze was built to level the playing field — aggregating foot traffic patterns, competitor density, and real-time demand signals into a single intuitive platform so that independent business owners can make the same caliber of location decisions that enterprise retailers have relied on for decades.
welcome.gradDesc: Localyze was developed as a capstone graduation project in the Computer Information Systems program. It demonstrates the integration of geospatial data processing, modern web technologies, and business intelligence design to solve real-world entrepreneurial challenges.
welcome.nextGreatLocation: Your next great location is waiting to be found.
welcome.runFirstScan: Run your first scan in minutes. No setup required.
welcome.footerTagline: Location intelligence for entrepreneurs who move fast and think smart.
welcome.fullFooter: © {{year}} Localyze · Final-year CIS graduation project · All data is illustrative.

steps.title: Welcome to Localyze!
steps.body: Localyze helps you analyze market viability for any business location in Amman. This quick tour will show you everything you need to get started.

steps.title: Navigate the App
steps.body: Use the sidebar on the left to move between sections: Dashboard for your overview, Scan to analyze locations, Reports to review saved scans, and Appendix for methodology details.

steps.title: Run a Market Scan
steps.body: In the Scan page, choose a business type and search radius, then click anywhere on the map to drop your pin. Hit "Run Analysis" to get your market report instantly.

steps.title: Read Your Results
steps.body: After scanning you'll see three key metrics: Feasibility Score (overall viability), Competitor Density (how many rivals are nearby), and Market Saturation (how crowded the market is).

steps.title: Get AI-Powered Insights
steps.body: Click "Get AI Suggestions" to generate a strategic analysis powered by Gemini AI — covering market risks, growth opportunities, and differentiation strategies tailored to your scan.

steps.title: Save & Track Reports
steps.body: Click "Save Report" in the scan panel after any analysis. All saved reports appear on your Dashboard and Reports page so you can compare locations over time.

appendix.title: Appendix and Methodology
appendix.subtitle: Definitions for every metric shown in Localyze, and an explanation of how each score is calculated from real data. Click any term to expand its full explanation.

scanSteps.title: Drop a pin & choose a business type
scanSteps.desc: You select a location on the map in Amman, a business type (e.g. café, gym, pharmacy), an optional sub-type (e.g. Specialty Coffee, Women-only gym), and a scan radius between 300 m and 5 km.

scanSteps.title: Competitor discovery via Google Places
scanSteps.desc: The backend queries Google Places Nearby Search within your radius and place type. For restaurants and cafés, results are cross-referenced against a Talabat restaurant database using fuzzy name matching (≥85% confidence) to filter by cuisine — so a search for "Sushi" only counts actual sushi venues, not generic restaurants.

scanSteps.title: District demographics lookup
scanSteps.desc: Your coordinates are matched to one of Amman's sub-districts using bounding-box geometry (falling back to nearest centroid). Each district carries census data: total population, youth (ages 15–34), elderly (60+), and male/female breakdown.

scanSteps.title: Education proximity check
scanSteps.desc: For food and beverage business types (café, restaurant, fast food, bakery), the system searches for universities within 1 km and validates them against a whitelist of known Jordanian universities — filtering out false positives like cafeteria entrances or parking lots near campuses.

scanSteps.title: Feasibility score computation
scanSteps.desc: All collected data is fed into a scoring algorithm (logistic curve + adjustments) and returned alongside raw competitor data, demographic stats, and rating summaries.

scanSteps.title: AI insights (optional)
scanSteps.desc: The full scan result is sent to Gemini 2.5 Flash with a structured business-consultant prompt. It returns a plain-language summary, key risks, and differentiation suggestions — all grounded in the actual competitor names and numbers from your scan.

appendix.feasibilityFormula: base = 100 / (1 + e^(-x))   where x = (ln(pop / comp) − ln(2000)) / 2
appendix.feasibilityDesc: The headline viability index for opening your chosen business at the selected location. It starts from a logistic (S-curve) function of the population-to-competitor ratio — specifically, how many people exist per competing business relative to a baseline of 2,000 people/competitor.
appendix.ratingQuality: Competitor rating quality: For every 0.1 stars below 4.0, the score increases by 1.5 points. Low-rated competitors signal a gap you can fill.
appendix.competitorReviewCount: A high average review count (entrenched competitors) penalises the score by up to −15 points.
appendix.priceLevel: Competitors priced above 2/4 add up to +5 pts per level — room for affordable positioning. Below 2 subtracts points.
appendix.educationProximity: Each university within 1 km of your pin adds +5 points (capped at +10). Relevant for F&B businesses that benefit from student foot traffic.
appendix.demographicFactor: Finally, the score is multiplied by a district-level demographic factor:
appendix.youthMultiplier: Most business types use the district's youth multiplier (based on % of 15–34 year-olds vs. the Amman average).
appendix.gymGenderWeight: Gyms additionally weight by gender ratio (male/female) when a gender sub-type is selected.
appendix.medicalElderlyMultiplier: Medical/pharmacy businesses use an elderly multiplier instead.
appendix.footTrafficDesc: A 0–100 proxy for the volume of potential customers in the area. Derived directly from the district's population density (people per km²). A density of 20,000 people/km² maps to the maximum score of 100.
appendix.footTrafficNote: It is one of two sub-bars shown inside the Feasibility Score card. It does not factor into the feasibility formula directly, but it is used to compute the Demand Signal.
appendix.demandSignalDesc: An estimate of demand strength that combines general footfall with student-driven demand. Foot traffic contributes 75% of the signal; each nearby university adds 8 points (capped at +25). A high Demand Signal alongside high Market Saturation indicates a contested but active market.
appendix.marketSatDesc: How crowded the market is, expressed as a percentage of a benchmark threshold of 50 similar businesses per km². At 50/km², the market is considered fully saturated (100%). Below 35% is considered low-risk; above 60% is saturated territory.
appendix.marketSatNote: Note: saturation reflects the density of competitors (per km² of your scan area), not the raw count. A larger radius covers more area, so raw competitor counts are not directly comparable across scans — density normalises for this.
appendix.competitorCountDesc: Total number of similar businesses found within your scan radius. Filtered by your chosen business type via the Google Places type field. For restaurants and cafés with a sub-type selected, the list is further filtered using Talabat category matching — cross-referencing competitor names against Talabat's Amman restaurant database via fuzzy matching (≥85% confidence threshold). If Talabat returns zero results for that cuisine, the system falls back to keyword search.
appendix.avgRatingDesc: The simple mean of all Google Maps star ratings across the competitors found. A low average (e.g. below 3.8) indicates the existing options are underperforming — an opportunity to capture market share with higher quality. A high average (e.g. above 4.3) signals entrenched, well-regarded competitors.
appendix.avgPriceLevelDesc: The mean of Google Places price_level values across competitors.
appendix.avgPriceLevelNote: A high average price level suggests competitors are premium — a potential opening for an affordable alternative. A low average may signal price pressure in the market. Not all Google Places listings have a price level set, so this average is computed only from listings that do.
appendix.compDensityDesc: The number of competitors divided by the area of your scan circle. This is the primary input into the Market Saturation calculation. A larger radius covers more area, so raw competitor counts are not directly comparable across scans — density normalises for this.
appendix.threatLevelDesc: Each competitor in the list is individually classified:
appendix.threatHighDesc: rating ≥ 4.3 AND more than 100 reviews. Established, well-rated, and popular. Hardest to displace.
appendix.threatMediumDesc: rating ≥ 3.8 AND more than 20 reviews. Reasonably good but not dominant.
appendix.threatLowDesc: everything else. Vulnerable to a well-executed offering.
appendix.districtDesc: Amman is divided into administrative sub-districts. Your pin's coordinates are matched to the correct district using boundary polygons. If coordinates fall outside all defined boundaries (e.g. edge-of-city pins), the system assigns the nearest district by centroid distance. Each district carries census-derived demographic data.
appendix.popDensityDesc: The total district population divided by the district's geographic area in km². Used directly to compute Foot Traffic. Districts with higher density (e.g. dense city-centre areas) produce a higher foot traffic score.
appendix.youthMarketDesc: The percentage of the district's population aged 15–34, sourced from Jordan's national census data. This is the primary customer demographic for most F&B, fitness, and lifestyle businesses. A higher youth percentage leads to a higher youth multiplier applied to the Feasibility Score.
appendix.youthRankDesc: A comparative ranking of the district's youth concentration relative to all other Amman districts. Rank 1 = highest youth concentration in the city. Used to contextualise whether this district is a youth hotspot or more mixed.
appendix.eduHubsDesc: The number of accredited universities or colleges within 1 km of your pin. Verified against a curated whitelist of Jordanian higher-education institutions (both English and Arabic names) to exclude false positives. Each confirmed university adds up to +8 points to the Demand Signal (capped at +25 total), and contributes +5 to the raw Feasibility Score (capped at +10).
appendix.badgeThresholdsDesc: Every metric card shows a badge summarising the result at a glance. Here are the exact thresholds used.
appendix.strongMeaning: The location has strong fundamentals — healthy demand, limited quality competition, or favourable demographics.
appendix.moderateMeaning: Viable but competitive. Success will depend on differentiation and execution.
appendix.weakMeaning: High competition, low demand, or poor demographics for this business type at this location.
appendix.lowMeaning: Few direct competitors within the radius — relatively open market.
appendix.moderateCompMeaning: Normal competitive environment. Quality and positioning matter.
appendix.highMeaning: Crowded space. Requires strong differentiation to stand out.
appendix.lowRiskMeaning: Market has room — competitor density is well below the saturation benchmark.
appendix.moderateSatMeaning: Getting crowded but not saturated. A strong concept can still win here.
appendix.saturatedMeaning: Dense competition relative to area. Very hard to succeed without a clear niche.

dataSourceItems.name: Google Places API
dataSourceItems.detail: Competitor discovery — names, addresses, ratings, review counts, price levels, opening hours, and websites. Results are paginated up to 60 listings per query and cached for 1 hour.

dataSourceItems.name: Amman District Census Data
dataSourceItems.detail: District-level population data for Amman's sub-districts, including total population, age breakdowns (youth 15–34, elderly 60+), and gender ratios. Used for Foot Traffic, Demand Signal, and the demographic multiplier in the Feasibility Score.

dataSourceItems.name: Jordanian Universities Whitelist
dataSourceItems.detail: A manually curated list of accredited Jordanian universities in both English and Arabic, used to validate nearby-education results from Google Places and exclude non-academic false positives.

dataSourceItems.name: Google Gemini 2.5 Flash
dataSourceItems.detail: AI analysis model used to generate the plain-language insights panel. Receives the full structured scan result and returns a business-consultant-style summary, risk list, and differentiation suggestions.

dataSourceItems.name: Firebase / Firestore
dataSourceItems.detail: User authentication and cloud storage for saved reports. Reports are stored per user in the saved_reports collection with full scan results, scores, and AI analysis text.
